parameters.name: AccountGroupKey
parameters.description: The key of the account group for which the balance data is returned

parameters.name: AccountKey
parameters.description: The key of the account for which the balance data is returned

parameters.name: ClientKey
parameters.description: The key of the client for which the balance data is returned

parameters.name: FieldGroups
parameters.description: Specifies which data to return.

children.name: CalculateCashForTrading
children.description: Calculates cash available for trading from all accounts.

children.name: MarginOverview
children.description: Include instrument margin utilization for positions on a client, account group or an account.

parameters.name: AccountGroupKey
parameters.description: The key of the account group for which the margin overview is returned

parameters.name: AccountKey
parameters.description: The key of the account for which the margin overview is returned

parameters.name: ClientKey
parameters.description: The key of the client for which the margin overview is returned

endpoints.name: Get balance data for logged-in client

parameters.name: AccountFundingLimit
parameters.description: Account funding limit. This field is available for TaxSaving AccountType and FrancePea and FrancePeaPme SubAccountTypes.

parameters.name: AccountNetFundedAmount
parameters.description: Account funding limit. This field is available for TaxSaving AccountType and FrancePea and FrancePeaPme SubAccountTypes.

parameters.name: AccountRemainingFunding
parameters.description: Account Remaining Funding. This field is available for TaxSaving and Pension AccountTypes, and FrancePea and FrancePeaPme SubAccountTypes.

parameters.name: AccountValueProtectionLimit
parameters.description: If set, this value shields the account/client value from going below the given limit by automatically triggering closing of open positions should the limit be exceeded. A limit of zero means there is no limit.

parameters.name: CalculationReliability
parameters.description: In case an error was encountered while computing balances, this field indicates the source of the calculation error.

parameters.name: CashAvailableForTrading
parameters.description: Cash available for trading for the current account/client.

parameters.name: CashBalance
parameters.description: Current cash balance of the account/client.

parameters.name: CashBlocked
parameters.description: Cash blocked for the current account/client.

parameters.name: CashBlockedFromWithdrawal
parameters.description: Cash which cannot be withdrawn (e.g. outstanding tax)

parameters.name: ChangesScheduled
parameters.description: True if there are scheduled changes to margin, collateral or rating changes.

parameters.name: ClosedPositionsCount
parameters.description: Number of current closed positions.

parameters.name: CollateralAvailable
parameters.description: Sum of collateral from positions, cash, collateral credit and other holdings available to maintain positions.

parameters.name: CollateralCreditLine
parameters.description: The utilization state and maximum collateral credit line limit.

children.name: Exposure
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line.

children.name: UtilizationPct
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line in proportion to the line limit.

parameters.name: CollateralCreditValue
parameters.description: The utilization state and maximum collateral credit value.

children.name: Exposure
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line.

children.name: UtilizationPct
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line in proportion to the line limit.

parameters.name: CollateralLoan
parameters.description: Size of loan secured by collateral

parameters.name: CorporateActionUnrealizedAmounts
parameters.description: Corporate Action Unrealized Amounts.

parameters.name: CostToClosePositions
parameters.description: Estimated costs payable if all position where to be closed on the account/client. The provided value is negative for costs charged to the account.

parameters.name: CreditLine
parameters.description: The utilization state and maximum credit limit.

children.name: Exposure
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line.

children.name: UtilizationPct
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line in proportion to the line limit.

parameters.name: Currency
parameters.description: Currency of the Account, AccountGroup, or Client, depending on the selected entity for which balances are requested. All values provided in this response are denominated in this currency unless otherwise specified.

parameters.name: CurrencyDecimals
parameters.description: Number of decimals used to represent values in this currency. For most common currency denominations this value will be: 2.

parameters.name: FinancingAccruals
parameters.description: Net financing accruals (e.g. interest, carrying costs, and holding fees)

parameters.name: FundsAvailableForSettlement
parameters.description: Funds available for trades settling today.

parameters.name: FundsReservedForSettlement
parameters.description: Funds reserved for trades settling today at market opening.

parameters.name: InitialMargin
parameters.description: If set, this represents the calculation entity's initial margin (purchasing power).

children.name: CollateralAvailable
children.description: Sum of collateral from positions, cash, collateral credit and other holdings available for opening new positions.

children.name: MarginAvailable
children.description: Margin available for opening new positions. Initial margin available.

children.name: MarginCollateralNotAvailableIncludingCostToClosePositions
children.description: Current collateral deduction of unrealized positions with cost to close positions deducted.

children.name: MarginUsedByCurrentPositions
children.description: Sum of initial margin currently reserved.

children.name: MarginUtilizationPct
children.description: Level of margin already utilized in percent. I.e. the ratio between the margin collateral used and the total available margin collateral. Indicates distance to margin calls.

children.name: NetEquityForMargin
children.description: Value used as basis to calculate initial margin available.

children.name: OtherCollateralDeduction
children.description: Other Collateral Deduction derived from regulatory contributors margin value.

parameters.name: IntradayMarginDiscount
parameters.description: Margin credited for unnetted positions closed intraday

parameters.name: IsPortfolioMarginModelSimple
parameters.description: True if the given client/account is on simple margining. F.ex. Used to indicate if the account supports close all positions. Typically this means the client has a single account or single account margining and no settlement accounts. As well as no Fx Forwards/Options, listed options or Futures using advanced margining methods.

parameters.name: MarginAndCollateralUtilizationPct
parameters.description: Level of margin already utilized in percent. I.e. the ratio between the margin collateral used and the total available margin collateral. Indicates distance to margin calls.

parameters.name: MarginAvailableForTrading
parameters.description: Margin available for trading. The amount of remaining collateral available for margin trading for the account/client.

parameters.name: MarginCollateralNotAvailable
parameters.description: Current collateral deduction of unrealized positions.

parameters.name: MarginCollateralNotAvailableDetail
parameters.description: Detail of MarginCollateralNotAvailable

children.name: CBBCCategoryN
children.description: Callable Bull/Bear Contract Category N. Call price or level is equal to its strike price or level, under which you will not receive any cash payment after the occurrence of a mandatory call event, and will lose your entire investment.

children.name: CBBCCategoryR
children.description: Callable Bull/Bear Contract Category R. Call price or level is different from its strike price or level, and you may receive a residual cash payment (called "residual value") upon the occurrence of a mandatory call event. However, in the worst case, you will not receive any residual value and will lose your entire investment.

children.name: CertificateBarrierDiscount
children.description: A Discount Certificate with Barrier allows investors with moderate to high risk tolerance to buy a selected underlying asset at a discount. The more favorable entry price gives a yield advantage, so that an attractive return is possible even in a sideways market.

children.name: CertificateBarrierReverseConvertibles
children.description: With Barrier Reverse Convertibles investors with a moderate-to-high risk tolerance in sideways moving markets can optimize their opportunities to earn yields thanks to a coupon. As in the Reverse Convertibles, the coupon is always paid out. The type and amount of the repayment of the nominal amount on expiry is aligned to the price of the underlying asset (e.g. individual equity, share index, equity basket, currency pair or commodity).

children.name: CertificateBonus
children.description: Mirrors the price movement of the underlying only if and when the underlying price exceeds the defined barrier. If the certificate expires below the barrier, it offers partial protection/return of investment.

children.name: CertificateCapitalProtectionWithCoupon
children.description: Capital protection certificates with coupon, also known as Coupon CPNs, are capital protected at maturity. This means that at the end of the usually multi-year term, you will always receive at least 90% of the nominal amount back. Depending on the features, the capital protection is usually between 90% and 100% of the nominal value.

children.name: CertificateCapitalProtectionWithKnockOut
children.description: Capital Protection Certificates with Barrier, also known as Barrier CPNs or Dolphin CPNs, are capital protected at maturity. The capital protection usually amounts to 90 - 100 percent of the nominal value, so that at maturity you will be paid back at least 90% of the capital invested at issue - regardless of the underlying asset.

children.name: CertificateCappedCapitalProtected
children.description: Guarantees a capped percentage increase of the underlying asset's value above the issue price at expiry/maturity. Max loss is the amount invested multiplied by the CapitalProtection percentage.

children.name: CertificateDiscount
children.description: Yields a capped return if the underlying asset's value is above the specified cap level at expiry. If the underlying's value is below the strike at expiry, the investor received the underlying or equivalent value. Offers direct exposure in underlying at a lower price (discount) with a capped potential profit and limited loss.

children.name: CertificateOtherCapitalProtection
children.description: Capital protection products are the most defensive form of Investment Products and are therefore suitable for investors with a low to moderate risk tolerance.

children.name: CertificateOtherConstantLeverage
children.description: Constant Leverage Certificates, aka Factor Certificates, enable investors who are willing to take risks to participate disproportionately in the price performance of an underlying asset (e.g. equities, indices, commodities or currency pairs)

children.name: CertificateOtherParticipation
children.description: Participation products are covered by Tracker Certificates. These Certificates are suitable for investors with a medium to high risk preference who want to participate cost efficiently and unlimitedly in the price development of a single stock, a market or a market segment.

children.name: CertificateOtherYieldEnhancement
children.description: Investment Products aimed at optimizing yields offer partial protection. This can provide attractive returns even when the price of the respective underlying asset just remains stationary up to maturity.

children.name: CertificateOutperformanceBonus
children.description: With Bonus-Outperformance Certificates, you can apply leverage, since Bonus-Outperformance Certificates offer you the chance to participate disproportionately and without restriction in rising prices of the underlying asset (e.g. an equity, an index or a currency pair). This means that as of a predefined price level (the so-called strike level) participation in a potentially rising price performance is higher than 100%. The exact performance rate varies depending on the product features and is fixed per issue.

children.name: CertificateReverseConvertibles
children.description: With Reverse Convertibles investors with a moderate-to-high risk tolerance in sideways moving markets can optimize their opportunities to earn yields thanks to a coupon. While the coupon is always paid out, the type and amount of the repayment of the nominal amount on expiration is aligned to the price of the underlying asset (e.g. individual equity, share index, equity basket, currency pair or commodity).

children.name: CertificateTracker
children.description: A certificate that mirrors the price movement of the underlying instrument. Often used to trade movements in indicies. Movements can be a fixed ratio of the underlying and can be inverted for bearish/short speculation. Risk is equivalent to owning the underlying.

children.name: CertificateTwinWin
children.description: With Twin-Win Certificates, you can pull off a remarkable balancing act on the markets, i.e. whether the underlying asset (e.g. equity or index) rises or falls, you can profit in both cases. In doing so, you participate 1:1 in price gains of the underlying asset over and above the strike price without restriction (taking into account the subscription ratio). Price losses below the strike price are, on the other hand, converted accordingly on the due date if the price of the underlying asset has never fallen to or below a specific barrier (Knock-Out Level) during the term. However, after a barrier has been broken the conversion of prices losses of the underlying asset into gains ceases to apply. Instead, in this case Twin-Win Certificates behave like conventional Tracker Certificates, so that price losses of the underlying asset (under the strike price) lead to corresponding losses in the certificate.

children.name: CertificateUncappedCapitalProtection
children.description: Guarantees a percentage increase of the underlying asset's value above the issue price at expiry/maturity. Max loss is the amount invested multiplied by the CapitalProtection percentage.

children.name: CertificateUncappedOutperformance
children.description: Provides leveraged returns when the underlying price exceeds the threshold strike price. The amount leverage is defined by the Participation %. When the underlying is below the strike price, the certificate mirrors the underlying price 1:1.

children.name: CfdOnCompanyWarrant
children.description: Cfd on unlisted warrant issued by a corporation.

children.name: CompanyWarrant
children.description: Unlisted warrant issued by a corporation, often physically settled.

children.name: GuaranteeNote
children.description: Danish investment scheme (“Grantbevis”). Not online tradeable.

children.name: InlineWarrant
children.description: Inline Warrants. Holders receive a pre-determined amount that depends on whether an underlying asset falls at, or within or outside the upper and lower strikes at expiry.

children.name: PortfolioNote
children.description: Danish pooled investment scheme (“Pulje”). Not online tradeable.

children.name: SrdOnEtf
children.description: SRD. (Service de Règlement Différé) on Etf.

children.name: SrdOnStock
children.description: SRD. (Service de Règlement Différé) on Stock.

children.name: WarrantOtherLeverageWithKnockOut
children.description: In the case of Warrants with Knock-Out, aka Turbos, the name says it all. With these investment instruments, investors who are willing to take risks can shift into turbo and considerably increase their yield potential.

children.name: WarrantOtherLeverageWithoutKnockOut
children.description: Warrants allow investors who are willing to take risks can choose between Call and Put Warrants. Call Warrants benefit from rising prices of the underlying asset whereas. Put Warrants rely on falling prices of the underlying asset.

children.name: ContributingAssetTypes
children.description: Valid AssetTypes for this contributor. Note that as a contributor can be an aggregate of several positions, this is a list of AssetTypes.

children.name: InitialCollateral
children.description: Initial collateral available of the instrument

children.name: InitialCollateralNotAvailable
children.description: Initial collateral not available of the instrument

children.name: MaintenanceCollateral
children.description: Maintenance collateral available of the instrument

children.name: MaintenanceCollateralNotAvailable
children.description: Maintenance collateral not available of the instrument

parameters.name: MarginCollateralNotAvailableIncludingCostToClosePositions
parameters.description: Current collateral deduction of unrealized positions with cost to close positions deducted.

parameters.name: MarginExposureCoveragePct
parameters.description: Percentage of total margin exposure to total collateral. I.e. the ratio between the Account Value and the Net Exposure. If account exposure is 0, the coverage percent is also returned as 0.

parameters.name: MarginNetExposure
parameters.description: Total net exposure of margin traded positions across all instruments.

parameters.name: MarginOverview
parameters.description: Instrument margin utilization for positions.

children.name: Groups
children.description: List of AssetType groups with it's contributing positions and total margin utilization.

children.name: Contributors
children.description: Contributors are either a list of instruments or a single (anonymous) object representing implicitly aggregated contributors

children.name: DisplayHint
children.description: Hint to the client application about how it should display the instrument.

children.name: CryptoCurrencies
children.description: Crypto Currencies. Intended to be used for Forex Spot / crypto currency crosses..

children.name: Forex
children.description: Forex. Intended to be used for Cfds on Futures on Forex.

children.name: Interests
children.description: Interest rates. Intended to be used for Cfds on Futures on bonds.

children.name: None
children.description: Indicates not special display hint is required.

children.name: StockIndices
children.description: Stock indices. Intended to be used for Cfds on Futures on stock indices.

children.name: InstrumentDescription
children.description: Description of instrument. Not available for futures.

children.name: InstrumentSpecifier
children.description: Instrument identifier. Base contract for futures and symbol for other instruments.

children.name: Uic
children.description: Uic identifying the instrument, if any

children.name: GroupType
children.description: AssetType group - used by margin calculation.

children.name: CFDs
children.description: Margin impact of CFDs - presumably all categories

children.name: FuturesAndOptions
children.description: Margin impact of future and option contract instruments

children.name: Other
children.description: Margin overview group based on unspecified criteria

children.name: TotalMargin
children.description: Total margin impact of the group of instrument.

parameters.name: MarginUsedByCurrentPositions
parameters.description: Sum of maintenance margin used for current positions on the account/client. The provided value is negative for any margin that is charged to the available margin on the account/client.

parameters.name: MarginUtilizationPct
parameters.description: Level of margin already utilized in percent. I.e. the ratio between the margin collateral used and the total available margin collateral. Indicates distance to margin calls.

parameters.name: NetEquityForMargin
parameters.description: Value used as basis to calculate maintinance margin. It is calculated as TotalValue plus pending settlements (funds awaiting settlement for partners), minus MarginCollateralNotAvailable.

parameters.name: NetPositionsCount
parameters.description: Number of current open net positions.

parameters.name: NonMarginPositionsValue
parameters.description: Sum of MarketValue for all non-margin instruments held in the account/by the client.

parameters.name: OpenIpoOrdersCount
parameters.description: Open Ipo order(s) count

parameters.name: OpenPositionsCount
parameters.description: Number of current open positions.

parameters.name: OptionPremiumsMarketValue
parameters.description: Combined market value of premium for all options, both FX and Contract Options.

parameters.name: OrdersCount
parameters.description: Number of current open orders.

parameters.name: OtherCollateral
parameters.description: Indicates the value of securities that have been deposited as collateral for margin.

parameters.name: OtherCollateralDeduction
parameters.description: Other Collateral Deduction derived from regulatory contributor margin value.

parameters.name: SettlementLine
parameters.description: The utilization state and maximum settlement credit limit.

children.name: Exposure
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line.

children.name: UtilizationPct
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line in proportion to the line limit.

parameters.name: SettlementValue
parameters.description: Net current settlement value, long and short positions combined.

parameters.name: SpendingPowerDetail
parameters.description: Available spending power on the account/client.

children.name: Maximum
children.description: Spending power for shares / ETF (based on max collateral factor defined for shares)

parameters.name: TotalRiskLine
parameters.description: The utilization state and risk credit limit.

children.name: Exposure
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line.

children.name: UtilizationPct
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line in proportion to the line limit.

parameters.name: TotalValue
parameters.description: Current value of unrealized positions incl. cash balance and transactions not booked, deducted by cost to close positions unless is enabled.

parameters.name: TradingLine
parameters.description: The utilization state and maximum trading credit limit.

children.name: Exposure
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line.

children.name: UtilizationPct
children.description: The current utilization of the credit line in proportion to the line limit.

parameters.name: TransactionsNotBooked
parameters.description: Value of transactions that have yet to be booked to the account/client.

parameters.name: TransactionsNotBookedDetail
parameters.description: Detail of transaction(s) that yet have to be booked to the account/client.

children.name: Accrual
children.description: Net accrual for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: AdditionalTransactionCost
children.description: Net additional transaction cost for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: BondValue
children.description: Net bond value for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: CashDeposit
children.description: Net cash deposit for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: CashReservation
children.description: Net cash reservation for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: CashWithdrawal
children.description: Net cash withdrawal for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: CertificatesValue
children.description: Net value of certificates for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: Commission
children.description: Net commission for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: ExchangeFee
children.description: Net exchange fee for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: ExternalCharges
children.description: Net external charges for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: FundsReservedByOrder
children.description: Net funds reserved by order for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: IpoSubscriptionFee
children.description: IPO subscription fee for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: LeveragedKnockOutProductsValue
children.description: Net value of leveraged knock-out products (Turbos) for the transaction(s) not booked yet.

children.name: MutualFundValue
children.description: Net mutual fund value for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: OptionPremium
children.description: Net option premium for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: ShareValue
children.description: Net share value for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: StampDuty
children.description: Net stamp duty for the transaction(s) not booked yet

children.name: WarrantPremium
children.description: Net warrant premium for the transaction(s) not booked yet

parameters.name: TriggerOrdersCount
parameters.description: Number of current open trigger orders.

parameters.name: UnrealizedMarginClosedProfitLoss
parameters.description: ProfitLoss on all closed (netting and explicit) part of positions (Intraday netting specific).

parameters.name: UnrealizedMarginOpenProfitLoss
parameters.description: ProfitLoss on open part of positions (Intraday netting specific).

parameters.name: UnrealizedMarginProfitLoss
parameters.description: Sum of profit/loss ex. costs for all margin instruments held in the account/by the client.

parameters.name: UnrealizedPositionsValue
parameters.description: The current unrealized profit/loss and face value of all positions excl. costs.

parameters.name: UnrealizedPositionsValueExcludingCostToClosePositions
parameters.description: The current unrealized profit/loss and face value of all positions excl. cost to close positions.

parameters.name: VariationMargin
parameters.description: The change in value of unrealized derivatives positions since opening them.

parameters.name: VariationMarginCashBalance
parameters.description: Cash amount withdrawn from unrealized derivatives positions (VariationMargin).

parameters.name: VariationMarginThreshold
parameters.description: Maximmum amount that may be withdrawn from unrealized derivatives positions (VariationMargin).

endpoints.name: Create a subscription on a balance

parameters.name: Arguments
parameters.description: Arguments for the subscription request.

children.name: AccountGroupKey
children.description: The key of the account group for which the balance data is returned

children.name: AccountKey
children.description: The key of the account for which the balance data is returned

children.name: ClientKey
children.description: The key of the client for which the balance data is returned

children.name: CalculateCashForTrading
children.description: Calculates cash available for trading from all accounts.

children.name: MarginOverview
children.description: Include instrument margin utilization for positions on a client, account group or an account.

parameters.name: ContextId
parameters.description: The streaming context id that this request is associated with. This parameter must only contain letters (a-z) and numbers (0-9) as well as - (dash) and _ (underscore). It is case insensitive. Max length is 50 characters.

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: Optional Media type (RFC 2046) of the serialized data updates that are streamed to the client. Currently only application/json and application/x-protobuf is supported. If an unrecognized format is specified, the subscription end point will return HTTP status code 400 - Bad format.

parameters.name: ReferenceId
parameters.description: Mandatory client specified reference id for the subscription. This parameter must only contain alphanumberic characters as well as - (dash) and _ (underscore). Cannot start with _. It is case insensitive. Max length is 50 characters.

parameters.name: RefreshRate
parameters.description: Optional custom refresh rate, measured in milliseconds, between each data update. Note that it is not possible to get a refresh rate lower than the rate specified in the customer service level agreement (SLA).

parameters.name: ReplaceReferenceId
parameters.description: Reference id of the subscription that should be replaced.

parameters.name: Tag Obsolete
parameters.description: : Optional client specified tag used for grouping subscriptions.

endpoints.name: Remove all subscriptions marked with a specific tag

parameters.name: ContextId
parameters.description: Unique streaming context ID part of the streaming session.

parameters.name: Tag
parameters.description: Tag that subscriptions are marked with.

parameters.name: ContextId
parameters.description: Unique streaming context ID part of the streaming session.

parameters.name: ReferenceId
parameters.description: Unique ID of the subscription
